statement.en: Infinite splitting in the syzygies of quaternionic groups
statement.summary: n+1 F n n F n-1 n-1 . . . . . .1 F 0 R 0) be a free resolution over the group ring R[] where R is commutative and is finite.The n th syzygy R[] nis the stable class of Im( n ) and has a tree structure with roots which do not extend infinitely downwards.We show that R[Q8p] 3 has infinitely many isomorphically distinct modules at the minimal level when R = Z[C ] is the integral group ring of the infinite cyclic group and Q 8p is the quaternion group of order 8p where p 3 is prime.This poses severe difficulties in attempting to solve the D(2) problem of CTC Wall for the groups C Q 8p

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The manuscript appears to present a novel technical result (infinite splitting in quaternionic syzygies) that has not yet undergone peer review. The claim concerns established algebraic structures (group rings, resolutions, syzygies) but the specific theorem about R[Q8p]₃ infiniteness is dated 2026-04-25 and categorized as cs.AI rather than math.AT or math.GR, suggesting preprint status or potential miscategorization. The result is mathematically well-formed but validation pending.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 素数をパラメータとする三次のシジジーが無限に分裂し、同型類が果てしなく増殖する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 根から下方へ無限に枝分かれする樹構造が、同型類の海へと溶けていく図。
